name: Trade Book
summary: Retrieves the user's Trade Book via a POST call. The request sends a jData JSON object (uid, actid) plus the jKey login token; on success the response is a JSON array of trade/order objects (one per fill).

request_params.name: jData
request_params.description: Should send json object with fields in below list.

json_fields.name: actid
json_fields.description: Account Id of logged in user

response_fields.name: stat
response_fields.description: Order book success or failure indication.

response_fields.name: tsym
response_fields.description: Trading symbol / contract on which order is placed.

response_fields.name: qty
response_fields.description: Order Quantity [If qty is junk value other than numbers].

response_fields.name: prd
response_fields.description: Display product alias name, using prarr returned in user details.

response_fields.name: trantype
response_fields.description: B -> BUY, S -> SELL [transtype should be 'B' or 'S' else reject].

response_fields.name: fillshares
response_fields.description: Total Traded Quantity of this order

response_fields.name: avgprc
response_fields.description: Average trade price of total traded quantity

response_fields.name: remarks
response_fields.description: Any message Entered during order entry.

response_fields.name: ret
response_fields.description: Order validity [ret should be DAY / EOS / IOC else reject]

notes:
- Base URL is https://piconnect.flattrade.in/PiConnectAPI ; the curl sample uses the placeholder 'https://BaseURL/TradeBook'.
- Request body is form-style: jData={...JSON...}&jKey=... with Content-Type application/json (as shown verbatim in the doc).
- The sample request's jData has a trailing comma after the "actid" line (invalid strict JSON) - reproduced verbatim from the doc.
- Success response is a JSON ARRAY of trade objects (one per fill/order); each object's 'stat' is 'Ok'. Failure response is a single JSON object with stat='Not_Ok'.
- The failure JSON block is NOT printed literally in the source; it is reconstructed from the documented failure-fields table (stat/request_time/emsg). Values shown are the doc's description placeholders, not real data.
- In the response field table the cells for 'uid' and 'actid' are BLANK (no possible-value or description) in the Trade Book section - kept blank to stay faithful to the source (the order-entry / Positions Book 'User Id' / 'Account Id' wording is NOT carried over here).
- In the response field table, 'qty', 'trantype', and 'ret' carry a trailing '*' in the image. The '*' is a required-input marker carried over from the order-entry field table; in this response context it is informational only.
- The sample success objects include keys not present in the documented response field table: prc, norentm, exch_tm (present in the JSON, undocumented in the field list).
- Possible-value cells bound from the page image: stat=Ok or Not_Ok, trantype=B / S, prctyp=LMT, ret=DAY / EOS / IOC.
- Page 27 begins the next section (POSITIONS BOOK); the Trade Book content itself spans pages 25-26. (Note: page 25 top also carries the tail of the previous Order Book section's failure-field table.)
issues: The failure-response JSON is not printed verbatim in the source (only the field table is shown), so sample_failure_response is reconstructed from that table with the doc's placeholder text. The page-26 response table lists 'uid' and 'actid' rows with blank possible-value/description cells; these are kept blank rather than inferred from the Positions Book table. The 'qty', 'trantype', and 'ret' rows carry a '*' in the response table that is a carried-over required-input marker, not a response requirement.
